channel.title: Research Projects on Harish Baki
channel.description: Recent content in Research Projects on Harish Baki

item.title: DFS Project
item.description:
Organization: Collaborative Forecasting Program
Period: 2025-Present

Applied atmospheric forecasting collaboration for model development and performance improvement.

item.title: EU-SCORES (TU Delft)
item.description:
Organization: TU Delft
Period: 2022-2024

Contributed to EU-SCORES (European Scalable Offshore Renewable Energy Sources), a €45M Horizon 2020 initiative led by the Dutch Marine Energy Centre. The project demonstrated integrated offshore multi-source energy parks combining wind, wave, and solar technologies to improve grid stability and reduce levelized cost of energy.

My role involved atmospheric and wind-resource modeling to support siting, performance estimation, and energy yield assessments for offshore co-located renewable systems across European coastal regions.

item.title: WISER Project
item.description:
Organization: University at Albany
Period: 2024-2025

Contributing to WISER (Center for Weather Innovation and Smart Energy and Resilience), an NSF-funded Phase 1 Industry-University Cooperative Research Center (IUCRC) between the University at Albany and the University of Connecticut. The center bridges meteorological research and the energy industry to improve power grid efficiency, reliability, and resilience under changing climate conditions.

My work focuses on weather and forecasting model development, supporting research thrusts in outage and load resilience modeling and next-generation atmospheric forecasting for energy applications.
